name: Conimbricense Portuguese

sandhi_rules.name: final-s-prevocalic-voicing
sandhi_rules.notes: Coimbra realisation of the external prevocalic /s/-sandhi. A word-final coda /s/ (surfacing [ʃ] in isolation and pre-consonantally via the inherited coda 'chiado') is realised as the POST-ALVEOLAR [ʒ] before a vowel-initial following word: os olhos -> [ˈuʒ ˈoʎuʃ], vamos para as aulas -> [... ɐʒ ˈawlɐʃ]. Re-declared here by id (OVERLAY_BY_ID) to override the inherited base [z]; it fires ONLY before a vowel. SOURCING: this palatal prevocalic realisation is demonstrated NATIVELY by the Coimbra speaker Lúcio (conimbricense de gema, professor de português) in Portuguese With Leo, 'Coimbra tem sotaque?' (native-speaker / popular-linguistics, NOT academic; https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=R4yUi68VHFg): asked how he links 'os olhos', he answers 'Nós faríamos a junção com o som de J e então seria os olhos', while the Lisbon host uses [z] ('os olhos' with the /z/ of casa). VARIABLE: the feature is explicitly variable in the centre — Lúcio himself says 'esta é uma coisa que varia muito' and Leo notes the [z] realisation 'está muito mais próxima do que explica a gramática' (i.e. [z] is the standard pole); the neutral Coimbra centre alternates [z]~[ʒ] and this spec models the marked local [ʒ] pole. The [ʒ] extension into the centre-north is corroborated by the Trás-os-Montes speaker (Portuguese With Leo, 'Trás-os-Montes', https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=70a_vNEa_aw), who says of Coimbra 'às vezes sai-lhe, outras vezes não, é assim meio misturado'. The standard/Lisbon literature gives [z] here (Mateus & d'Andrade 2000: ch.2); a page-pinned academic source for the Coimbra prevocalic [ʒ] specifically was not located. The Algarve is the strongest/categorical [ʒ] variety and São Miguel shares it (see pt-PT-x-algarve, pt-PT-x-sao-miguel); Lisbon and the (western) North keep [z].

notes: Conimbricense (Coimbra) European Portuguese — the central variety widely considered 'o sotaque mais neutro de Portugal' (Portuguese With Leo, 'Coimbra tem sotaque?', native-speaker source, https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=R4yUi68VHFg; the Coimbra guest Lúcio: 'aquele que em teoria segue as regras da fonética'). A DELTA spec over the broad pt-PT base: Coimbra is modelled as the CONSERVATIVE central norm that keeps the base values and deliberately does NOT take the Lisbon/Estremenho innovations, with one marked local delta (the [ʒ] prevocalic sandhi). DELIBERATELY-INHERITED (kept as the neutral base value, NOT re-declared, so Coimbra diverges from Lisbon precisely by NOT innovating): (1) NO pre-palatal centralisation of stressed <e> — Coimbra keeps the mid front vowel before a palatal (coelho -> [ˈkɔɛʎu], espelho, vermelho, Fonte da Telha) where Lisbon centralises to [ɐ] ('coâlho', 'vermalho'); Lúcio contrasts exactly these 'palavras acabadas em E-L-H-O / E-L-H-A' as 'das coisas que mais distinguem o sotaque de Coimbra do sotaque de Lisboa', saying Coimbra 'fala da forma que teoricamente é o correto'. Because Coimbra inherits pt-PT (which lacks the Lisbon LX_STRESSED_E_PREPALATAL rule), the mid vowel is kept automatically — no rule is added. This same conservative [e] is corroborated for the neighbouring North by the Trás-os-Montes speaker ('vermelho eu digo vermelho, Lisboa vermalho'; https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=70a_vNEa_aw). (2) <ei> -> [ej] kept — Coimbra keeps the [ej] diphthong (the base default), described by Lúcio as intermediate between the Lisbon [ɐj] ('menos A') and the very strong Porto [ej] ('não tanto E'): fevereiro -> [fɨvɨˈɾejɾu], NOT the Lisbon [ɐj]. (3) <ou> -> [o] monophthong — like Lisbon and the whole Centre-South and UNLIKE the North: Lúcio 'a palavra pouco nós falamos pouco, a palavra roupa nós falamos roupa, igual a Lisboa'. This is the base default, kept. (4) Unstressed /i/ RETAINED — Coimbra pronounces the unstressed [i] that Lisbon cuts (Filipa -> [fiˈlipɐ] not the Lisbon 'Flipa'; piscina, disciplina): Lúcio 'nós não pronunciamos o I [Lisbon], vocês [Coimbra] pronunciam o I; no Norte também se pronuncia mais este I'. This is the base value (the Lisbon deletion is not modelled), kept. MODELLED DELTA: the [ʒ] prevocalic external /s/-sandhi (variable [z]~[ʒ]; see the PT_FINAL_S_PREVOCALIC_VOICE override), the one feature by which Coimbra departs from the base [z]. NOT MODELLED (documented, not faked): the OI~OU lexical variation (Coimbra prefers the <oi> spelling/pronunciation toiro/loiça/loiro where Lisbon prefers <ou> touro/louça — Lúcio 'Nós falamos com o ditongo OI: toiro, loiça, loiro') is an orthographic-lexical doublet choice, not a regular grapheme rule, and is left to the lexicon. All other EP processes (unstressed vowel reduction, dark coda /l/ [ɫ], coda-sibilant chiado, stress) are inherited unchanged from pt-PT.

ancestors.notes: Broad descriptive European Portuguese standard. Coimbra is the conservative central norm: it keeps the base values (no pre-palatal [ɐ], [ej] kept, [o] for <ou>, unstressed /i/ retained) where Lisbon innovates, and adds only the marked local [ʒ] prevocalic sandhi as a delta.

sources.author: Portuguese With Leo (with Lúcio, conimbricense)
sources.title: Coimbra tem sotaque?
sources.notes: Native-speaker EP accent deep-dive (NOT academic). Coimbra called 'o sotaque mais neutro de Portugal' / 'aquele que em teoria segue as regras da fonética'. Attests, with example words: the conservative mid vowel before a palatal (coelho/espelho/vermelho/Telha, 'das coisas que mais distinguem o sotaque de Coimbra do sotaque de Lisboa'); <ei> as [ej] intermediate between Lisbon [ɐj] and Porto [ej] (fevereiro); <ou> monophthong [o] like Lisbon (pouco, roupa); retention of unstressed /i/ (Filipa, piscina, disciplina, where Lisbon cuts it); and the [ʒ] prevocalic external /s/-sandhi ('os olhos' with the J-sound), explicitly variable ('varia muito').

sources.author: Mateus, M.H.M. & d'Andrade, E.
sources.title: The Phonology of Portuguese
sources.notes: Reference EP phonology for the inherited base processes (unstressed vowel reduction, dark coda /l/, coda sibilants) that Coimbra inherits unchanged from pt-PT. Standard EP gives the prevocalic external final /s/ as [z]; the Coimbra [ʒ] is a marked variable delta over this base.

sources.author: Cintra, Luís F. Lindley
sources.title: Nova proposta de classificação dos dialectos galego-portugueses
sources.publisher: Boletim de Filologia (Centro de Estudos Filológicos, Lisboa), vol. 22, pp. 81-116
sources.notes: Foundational classification of the Galician-Portuguese dialect space. Coimbra sits in the conservative central zone; the pedagogical/reference norm of European Portuguese is often anchored to educated central (Coimbra) speech, which lacks several Lisbon/Estremenho innovations (the [ɐj] value of <ei>, the pre-palatal centralisation) — the basis for modelling Coimbra as the base-conservative variety here.

stress.notes: Acordo Ortográfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam). DIPHTHONGS: declared so the bundled syllabifier counts nuclei correctly — without them every vowel run merges into one nucleus and co-e-lho is read as coe-lho, one syllable short, putting the stress and its vowel reduction on the wrong vowel. ⟨oe⟩ is hiatus, not a diphthong (Cunha & Cintra, §Ditongos).

location.notes: Coimbra — the city the Conimbricense variety is named for. [Wikidata Q45412 P625]
